Section 157(a): amended, on 26 November 2018, by section 459 of the Social Security Act 2018 (2018 No 32).
Section 157(a)﻿(i)﻿(A): repealed, on 1 October 2019, by section 32 of the Kāinga Ora–Homes and Communities Act 2019 (2019 No 50).

part.heading: Regulatory authority

history.history-note: Part 10: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Heading: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Interpretation of terms used in this Part

para.text: In this Part, unless the context otherwise requires,—
appeal body means the body specified in regulations made under section 187 to whom appeals under section 184 may be made
prescribed means prescribed in regulations made under section 190

prescribed eligibility criteria means the eligibility criteria prescribed under section 190(1)﻿(d) that a community housing provider must meet to be registered or obtain registration of a specified class

prescribed performance standards means the performance standards prescribed under section 190(1)﻿(e) that a community housing provider must meet to maintain registration or registration of a specified class
register means the register of community housing providers established under section 172.

history.history-note: Section 158: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

part.crosshead: Appointment, objectives, functions, and operation of authority

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Regulatory authority

para.text: The Governor-General may by Order in Council made on the recommendation of the joint Ministers appoint a department, any specified business unit within a department, departmental agency, or a Crown entity to perform the functions and exercise the powers of the regulatory authority under this Part.
If the authority is a Crown entity, then the Crown Entities Act 2004 applies to the authority except to the extent that this Act expressly provides otherwise.
An order under this section is secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 159: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 159(3): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Authority's main objectives

para.text: The authority’s main objectives are—
to register and regulate community housing providers, in order to ensure that their tenants are appropriately housed; and
to support the growth of a fair, efficient, and transparent community housing sector.

history.history-note: Section 160: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Authority's functions

para.text: The functions of the authority are to—
approve and register community housing providers in accordance with prescribed eligibility criteria and performance standards; and
suspend or revoke such registration where a community housing provider no longer meets the prescribed eligibility criteria and performance standards; and
maintain a register of registered community housing providers; and
recommend to the joint Ministers the making of regulations for the purposes of this Part; and
monitor and enforce compliance by registered community housing providers with any regulations made under this Part; and
comply with any directions from the joint Ministers in relation to the regulation of registered community housing providers; and
provide the joint Ministers with such information and reports about the carrying out of its functions under this Part as the joint Ministers may request; and
perform any other functions conferred on the authority by this Part.

history.history-note: Section 161: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Ministerial directions to authority

para.text: The joint Ministers and, if applicable, any other Minister responsible for the authority may give to the authority—
directions specifying how registered community housing providers may be eligible for the funding by way of payments under reimbursement agreements and tailored agreements, including,—
the type of housing units that may be funded during any specified period of time; and
the number of housing units that may be funded during any specified period of time; and
the location of housing units that may be funded during any specified period of time:
directions stating the terms and conditions on which Crown grants are to be made available to registered community housing providers:
directions stating the terms and conditions on which assets of the Crown or of a Crown entity are to be made available to registered community housing providers.
Before making any direction under this section, the joint Ministers and, if applicable, any other Minister responsible for the agency must consult—
the agency and the authority; and
any social housing provider that, in the Minister's opinion, will be materially affected by the direction.
The requirement in subsection (2)﻿(b) does not apply to the first directions to be made under this section.

The authority must, in carrying out functions under this Act, give effect to any directions that are given to it under this section and that are signed by the joint Ministers and if applicable, any other Minister responsible for the authority.
If the authority is a Crown entity,—
subsections (1) to (4) do not limit Part 3 of the Crown Entities Act 2004; and
if there is any inconsistency between the provisions of this Act and the Crown Entities Act 2004 in relation to a direction under this section, the provisions of the Crown Entities Act 2004 prevail.
As soon as practicable after giving a direction under this section, a Minister must publish it in the Gazette and present a copy of it to the House of Representatives.

history.history-note: Section 162: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 162(1)﻿(a): amended, on 28 May 2015, by section 10(a) of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).
Section 162(1)﻿(a)﻿(i): amended, on 28 May 2015, by section 10(b) of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).
Section 162(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii): amended, on 28 May 2015, by section 10(b) of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).
Section 162(1)﻿(a)﻿(iii): amended, on 28 May 2015, by section 10(b) of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).
Heading: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Application for registration

para.text: A community housing provider may apply to the authority to be registered under this Part.
An application must—
contain the prescribed information; and
be accompanied by any relevant prescribed fee.

history.history-note: Section 163: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Further information to be provided with registration application

para.text: The authority may require a community housing provider to provide any further information that the authority considers appropriate to an application for registration by that provider.
The authority may refuse an application for registration if the community housing provider does not provide the further information required within a reasonable time after the requirement is made.

history.history-note: Section 164: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Authority must decide application for registration

para.text: The authority must, as soon as practicable after receiving an application for registration that complies with section 163,—
decide whether to register the applicant; and
give the applicant written notice of its decision; and
specify in the decision the class or classes of registration that the applicant has obtained.

history.history-note: Section 165: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Approval of registration

para.text: The authority may approve a community housing provider as a registered community housing provider if the authority is satisfied that—
the community housing provider’s application for registration complies with this Part; and
any relevant prescribed fee has been paid; and
having regard to the prescribed eligibility criteria and any other relevant matter, registration is appropriate.
The authority must in approving a registered community housing provider, determine the particular class or classes of registration that the provider has obtained.
The authority must give effect to any approval of a community housing provider under subsection (1) by—
giving notice of the registration of that provider in the Gazette; and
entering that provider’s name in the register.

The effect of registration is that the community housing provider is, depending on the class or classes of registration it has obtained, eligible to receive any 1 or more of the following, on terms and conditions set by the joint Ministers:
funding by way of payments under reimbursement agreements and tailored agreements:
Crown grants:
assets of the Crown, or of a Crown entity.

history.history-note: Section 166: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 166(4)﻿(a): replaced, on 28 May 2015, by section 11 of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).

prov.heading: Refusal of registration

para.text: If the authority is not satisfied that the requirements for registration in section 166 have been met, the authority must refuse to register the community housing provider.
The authority must give the community housing provider a written notice of the reasons for the decision to refuse to register the provider under this Part.

history.history-note: Section 167: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Registration continuous so long as criteria continue to be met

para.text: The authority must assess at least annually, and may assess at any other time, whether a registered community housing provider continues to meet the prescribed eligibility criteria and prescribed performance standards.

A registered community housing provider that continues to meet those criteria and standards is entitled to the continuation of its registration and any specified class of registration that it has obtained.

history.history-note: Section 168: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Suspension of registration

para.text: If, after making an assessment under section 168, the authority determines that a registered community housing provider no longer meets the prescribed eligibility criteria and prescribed performance standards, the authority must—
suspend the provider’s registration until the provider satisfies the authority that it meets those criteria and standards; and
record the suspension in the register.
The authority must give the provider at least 14 days' written notice and the opportunity to be heard before suspending its registration.
During the period for which the provider’s registration is suspended,—
the provider may not take on any new tenants paying an income-related rent:
the provider may be paid under a reimbursement agreement or a tailored agreement, subject to the terms of the agreement:
the provider may not apply for any further Crown grants:
the provider may not apply for any further assets of the Crown or of a Crown entity.

If the provider does not satisfy the authority for the purposes of subsection (1)﻿(a) within 12 months after the suspension, or any further period that the authority may determine, the authority must revoke the provider’s registration in accordance with sections 170 and 171.

history.history-note: Section 169: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 169(3)﻿(b): replaced, on 28 May 2015, by section 12 of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).

prov.heading: When registration may be revoked

para.text: The authority may revoke the registration of a community housing provider under this Part if the authority is satisfied on reasonable grounds that the provider—
has failed, or is failing, to meet 1 or more of the prescribed eligibility criteria; or
has failed, or is failing, to meet 1 or more of the prescribed performance standards; or
has failed, or is failing, to comply with a lawful requirement of the authority under this Part; or
has ceased to operate as a community housing provider; or
is unable to pay its debts or to continue carrying on its business.
The authority may revoke the registration of a community housing provider under this Part on the written request of the community housing provider.
The authority may revoke the registration of a community housing provider whether or not that registration has been suspended under section 169.

history.history-note: Section 170: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Procedure for revocation

para.text: In any case where the authority revokes a community housing provider’s registration under section 170, the authority must—
give written notice to the provider specifying the effective date of the revocation; and
give notice of the revocation in the Gazette; and
remove that provider’s name from the register; and
give written notice to the agency of this as soon as practicable.
If the revocation is on any of the grounds referred to in section 170(1), the authority must—
give the provider at least 14 days' written notice and the opportunity to be heard before revoking the registration; and
give the provider a written notice of reasons for the decision.

The effect of revocation is that the provider is no longer eligible to receive any benefits associated with any specified class or classes of registration that the provider has obtained, and those benefits may, without limitation, include payments under reimbursement agreements or tailored agreements, Crown grants, or assets of the Crown or of a Crown entity.

history.history-note: Section 171: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 171(3): amended, on 28 May 2015, by section 13 of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).

prov.heading: Register of community housing providers

para.text: The authority must establish and maintain a register of community housing providers that includes the following information about each registered provider:
the current (and any former) full name and address and incorporation details (if applicable) of the provider; and
the full names and addresses and appointment details of the current (and former) members of its governing body (if applicable); and

in the case of a registered provider who obtains a class of registration that qualifies that provider as being eligible to receive Crown grants or assets of the Crown or a Crown entity, the full details of any land in respect of which the provider is or was a registered owner, or over which it holds or has held a lease, tenancy agreement, or licence of more than 3 years' duration, or at which it provides or has provided community housing services to the public; and
its registration details as a registered community housing provider; and
any prescribed information or documents.

Subsection (1)﻿(b) does not require the authority to include on the register information about a person whose membership on a provider’s governing body ended before the provider became a registered community housing provider, but the authority has discretion to include that information.

history.history-note: Section 172(1): inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 172(1)﻿(c): amended, on 13 January 2020, by section 4(1) of the Regulatory Systems (Housing) Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 64).
Section 172(1)﻿(c): amended, on 12 November 2018, by section 250 of the Land Transfer Act 2017 (2017 No 30).
Section 172(2): inserted, on 13 January 2020, by section 4(2) of the Regulatory Systems (Housing) Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 64).

prov.heading: Certain community housing providers deemed to be registered

para.text: A community housing provider that on the date of commencement of this section (commencement date) has entered into a Relationship and Grant Agreement with the Ministry of Business, Innovation, and Employment is, on and from the commencement date, deemed to be a registered community housing provider that has obtained a class of registration that qualifies the provider as being eligible to receive the income-related rent subsidy for 1 year after the commencement date.
A community housing provider may choose not to be a deemed registered community housing provider by notifying the authority.
To avoid doubt, this section does not affect a community housing provider's right to receive Crown grants under the agreement referred to in subsection (1).

history.history-note: Section 173: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

part.crosshead: Provisions relating to registered community housing providers

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Authority to monitor registered community housing providers

para.text: The authority—
must monitor the compliance of registered community housing providers with the prescribed eligibility criteria and prescribed performance standards; and
may require persons to supply information or produce documents for that purpose under section 178.

history.history-note: Section 174: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Reporting requirements of registered community housing providers

para.text: A registered community housing provider must provide reports on its operations to the authority—
annually in accordance with section 176; and
at any other time as required by the authority.
A report must be in the form approved by the authority.

history.history-note: Section 175: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Annual reports provided by registered community housing providers

para.text: The reports to be provided annually by a registered community housing provider must include—
a report setting out its performance against the prescribed performance standards; and
a report containing its financial statements and accounts in accordance with a direction from the authority under this section; and
any other reports that the authority may require.
The authority may give directions in relation to the contents of financial statements and accounts to be provided by registered community housing providers.
The authority must publish a direction under this section in the Gazette.
Reports must be provided to the authority in each year not more than 28 days after the annual general meeting of the registered community housing provider.

history.history-note: Section 176: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Complaints about registered community housing providers

para.text: Any person may complain to the authority alleging that a registered community housing provider has failed, or is failing, to meet prescribed eligibility criteria or prescribed performance standards.
As soon as practicable after receiving a complaint, the authority must—
inform the registered community housing provider concerned of the complaint; and
decide whether to accept or decline the complaint.
The authority must, immediately after making a decision under subsection (2),—
give written notice of the decision to the person who made the complaint and the registered community housing provider concerned; and
if the authority decides to accept the complaint, proceed to investigate the complaint.
The authority may decline to accept, and is not required to investigate, a complaint that it considers vexatious or frivolous.
Nothing in this section affects the rights and obligations of community housing providers and their tenants under the Residential Tenancies Act 1986.

history.history-note: Section 177: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Authority may require person to supply information or produce documents

para.text: If the authority considers it necessary or desirable for the purposes of performing or exercising its functions, powers, or duties under this Act, the authority may, by written notice served on any person, require the person—
to supply to the authority, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice, any information or class of information specified in the notice; or
to produce to the authority, or to a specified person named in the notice, any document or class of documents specified in the notice (within the time and in the manner specified in the notice); or

if necessary, to reproduce, or assist in reproducing, in usable form, information recorded or stored in any document or class of documents specified in the notice (within the time and in the manner specified in the notice).
Information supplied in response to a notice under subsection (1)﻿(a) must be—
given in writing; and
signed in the manner specified in the notice.
If a document is provided in response to a notice under subsection (1), the authority, or the specified person to whom the document is produced, may—
inspect and make records of that document; and
take copies of the document or extracts from the document.
In this section, specified person means—
an employee of the authority; or
another person to whom the authority has delegated the power to receive the relevant information.

history.history-note: Section 178: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

part.crosshead: Intervention powers in respect of registered community housing providers

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Use of intervention powers by authority

para.text: The intervention powers of the authority under this Part may be used only in relation to a registered community housing provider that has obtained a class of registration that qualifies the provider as being eligible to receive Crown grants or assets of the Crown or of a Crown entity.
The authority may use its intervention powers in relation to a registered community housing provider referred to in subsection (1) if—
there has been a failure by the provider to meet the prescribed eligibility criteria or prescribed performance standards; or
the provider fails to comply with a legal requirement or direction given by the authority; or

as a result of a change to the constitution or rules of the provider, or to the business activities carried on by the provider, the authority believes that the ability of the provider to do either or both of the following things is, or will be, adversely affected:
comply with the prescribed eligibility criteria and prescribed performance standards:
provide social rental housing or affordable rental housing to people.
The authority must not exercise a power under this Part unless it is satisfied that the exercise of the power—
is appropriate in the circumstances; and
accords with guidelines established under subsection (4).
The joint Ministers must establish guidelines for the exercise of the authority's powers under this Part.
Guidelines established under subsection (4) must be published in the Gazette.
The authority must make a copy of the current guidelines available to each registered community housing provider.

history.history-note: Section 179: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Authority may appoint to governing body of registered community housing provider

para.text: The authority, after consulting the governing body of a registered community housing provider and considering any nominations made by that governing body, may appoint 1 or more persons, whom the authority considers to be appropriately qualified, to that governing body.
The appointment may be in place of 1 or more existing members of the governing body or in addition to the existing members of the governing body.
If the appointment is in place of an existing member, the governing body must terminate the existing member's appointment.
An appointment or termination under this section has effect as if made in accordance with the constitution or rules of the registered community housing provider.
This section applies despite anything to the contrary in the constitution or rules of the registered community housing provider.
No appointment may be made to a governing body under this section that would result in the proportion of members appointed by the authority being greater than that of a minority.

history.history-note: Section 180: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Authority may give binding instructions to registered community housing provider

para.text: The authority may, after consultation with the governing body of a registered community housing provider, give binding instructions to the registered community housing provider or the members of its governing body.
The instructions may relate to—
the entering by the registered community housing provider into arrangements that the authority considers appropriate with 1 or more other registered community housing providers:
the appointment of an administrator to control and direct the registered community housing provider:
any other matter relating to the registered community housing provider that the authority thinks fit.
The instructions may specify—
the time within which a matter referred to in subsection (2) must be complied with; and
conditions to which that matter is subject, including a requirement for prior approval of the authority.
In giving an instruction or an approval under this section, the authority must have regard to the interests of the tenants of the registered community housing provider.
A registered community housing provider must comply with an instruction given to it under this section.
A member of the governing body of a registered community housing provider must comply with an instruction given to the members of the governing body under this section.
The authority may give more than 1 set of instructions to a registered community housing provider in relation to the same matter.

history.history-note: Section 181: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Heading: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Reporting obligations

para.text: The joint Ministers may, by notice to the authority, request that the authority inquire into, and report on, any matter relating to the community housing sector.

history.history-note: Section 182: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Heading: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Rights of appeal

para.text: This section and sections 184 to 188 apply to any decision of the authority made under this Part—
relating to the registration (including eligibility for registration) of a community housing provider:
appointing a person to the governing body of a registered community housing provider under section 180:
giving binding instructions to a registered community housing provider under section 181.

history.history-note: Section 183: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Community housing provider may appeal

para.text: A community housing provider may, in accordance with regulations made under section 187 and within 28 days after the decision was made, appeal against a decision referred to in section 183 if the authority—
has confirmed it (whether as originally made or as varied) under the process for the time being established by the authority to review such decisions; or
has no such process for the time being established.

history.history-note: Section 184: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Powers of appeal body

para.text: In determining the appeal, the appeal body has all the powers, duties, functions, and discretions the authority had in relation to the matter concerned and may—
confirm, modify, or reverse the decision of the authority or any part of it; or
refer all or any part of the matter back to the authority for further consideration, together with—
any directions it thinks just relating to the reconsideration; and
a written statement of its reasons for doing so.
Without limiting subsection (1), the appeal body may make an order requiring an entity—
to be registered in the register with effect from a specified date; or
to be restored to the register with effect from a specified date; or
to be removed from the register with effect from a specified date; or
to remain registered in the register.
The specified date may be a date that is before or after the order is made.
The appeal body may make any other order it thinks fit.
An order may be subject to any terms or conditions that the appeal body thinks fit.
The appeal body—
may award costs against the authority in respect of any appeal if—
it is allowed in whole or in part; or
all or any part of the matter is referred back to the authority for further consideration; and
may award costs against the appellant in respect of any appeal that is refused, if the appeal body believes that the appeal was frivolous or vexatious, or should not have been brought.
Subject to section 188, a decision of the authority that is the subject of the appeal stands pending determination of the appeal.

history.history-note: Section 185: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Where appeal body established by regulations

para.text: If regulations under section 187(1)﻿(a) establish a body to dispose of appeals under sections 183 to 188, any party to an appeal to the body who is dissatisfied with any determination of the body may appeal to the District Court and in that case,—
within 14 days after the date of the determination, the appellant must—
lodge a notice of appeal with the court; and
give a copy of the notice to every other party to the appeal:
the court or a Judge may, on the application of the appellant or intending appellant, extend any time prescribed or allowed under this section for the lodging of a notice of appeal:
except as provided by this section, the case must be dealt with in accordance with the rules of the District Court.

history.history-note: Section 186: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 186: amended, on 1 March 2017, by section 261 of the District Court Act 2016 (2016 No 49).

prov.heading: Regulations relating to appeals

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, make regulations—
establishing a body to dispose of appeals under section 184, prescribing how its members are to be appointed, and prescribing how it is to hear and dispose of appeals; or

providing that such appeals are to be made to the District Court, and providing that the District Court Act 2016 and the rules of the District Court apply, with or without modification, to the hearing and disposal of appeals.
Any body established under subsection (1)﻿(a) is a statutory board within the meaning of the Fees and Travelling Allowances Act 1951, and every member of it is entitled to—
remuneration by way of fees, salary, or allowances for the member's services as a member of it:
payment of travelling allowances and expenses in respect of time spent travelling in its service.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 187: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 187(1)﻿(b): amended, on 1 March 2017, by section 261 of the District Court Act 2016 (2016 No 49).
Section 187(3): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Appeal body may make interim order pending determination of appeal

para.text: At any time before the final determination of an appeal, the appeal body may make an interim order requiring an entity—
to be registered in the register from a specified date; or
to be restored to the register with effect from a specified date; or
to be removed from the register with effect from a specified date; or
to remain registered in the register.
The specified date may be a date that is before or after the order is made.
An interim order may be subject to any terms or conditions that the appeal body thinks fit.
If the appeal body refuses to make an interim order, the person or persons who applied for the order may, within 1 month after the date of the refusal, appeal to the High Court against the decision.
If an interim order is made under subsection (1), the authority must—
amend the register in accordance with the order as soon as is reasonably practicable after receiving the order; and
include a copy of the order in the register, unless the appeal body orders otherwise.

To enable the authority to fulfil the duties imposed by this section, the Registrar of the appeal body in which the interim order is made must send a copy of the order to the authority as soon as practicable.

history.history-note: Section 188: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Delegation of powers under this Part

para.text: The authority may not delegate a power under this Part, except to—
an employee of the authority; or
a person engaged by the authority under a contract for services providing for the person to exercise that power.

Despite subsection (1), if the authority is a department it may, in accordance with clauses 2 and 3 of Schedule 6 of the Public Service Act 2020, delegate any power under this Part to the chief executive of another department, or to an employee of the department.
A power delegated by the authority in accordance with subsection (1) or (2) may, with the consent of the authority, be further delegated,—
by the chief executive of a department or departmental agency, to an employee of that department or departmental agency; or
by an employee of the authority, to another employee of the authority.
If the authority delegates a power under this Part to a body corporate engaged by the authority under a contract for services,—
the body corporate cannot subdelegate it except to a person who is an employee of the body corporate; and
an employee of the body corporate to whom it is subdelegated cannot subdelegate it further.
This section overrides section 130(1) of the Companies Act 1993.

history.history-note: Section 189: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 189(2): amended, on 7 August 2020, by section 135 of the Public Service Act 2020 (2020 No 40).
Heading: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: Regulations relating to community housing providers

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council made on the recommendation of the joint Ministers, make regulations for all or any of the following purposes:
providing for the way in which applications for registration as a community housing provider must be made or the information that those applications must contain:
in relation to the registration of community housing providers, specify different classes of registration for different purposes, including for the purposes of receiving—
funding by way of payments under reimbursement agreements and tailored agreements:
Crown grants:
assets of the Crown or of a Crown entity:
specifying any relevant fee that applies to applications or the method by which it may be calculated:
prescribing the eligibility criteria that must be met to be registered or to obtain registration of a specified class, including—
financial viability requirements; and
requirements related to organisational capacity and capability:
prescribing the performance standards that must continue to be met to maintain registration or to maintain registration of a specified class, including matters that relate to—
governance and management:
financial viability:
tenancy management:
asset and property management:
prescribing procedures, requirements, and other matters for the register of community housing providers established and maintained under section 172, including matters that relate to—
the operation of the register:
the form of the register:
the information and documents that must be included on the register:
access to the register:
the location of, and hours of access to, the register:
search criteria for the register:
specifying—
the information that must be provided by persons who make complaints against a registered community housing provider; and
the way in which that information must be evaluated; and
the way in which decisions on whether to proceed with the complaint must be made and implemented:
specifying the date on which (in accordance with this Part, the calculation mechanism, and the terms of the contracts between the authority and registered community housing providers)—
the income-related rent subsidy will become available to some or all registered community housing providers; and
an income-related rent will be available to some or all tenants of registered community housing providers:
specifying the type or types of registered community housing providers to whom funding by way of payments under reimbursement agreements and tailored agreements may or may not be made available:
providing for any other matters contemplated by this Part, necessary for its administration, or necessary for giving it full effect.
Any fee prescribed under subsection (1)﻿(c) must be reasonable, having regard to the need to recover the costs incurred by the authority in performing its functions under this Part.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 190: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Section 190(1)﻿(b)﻿(i): replaced, on 28 May 2015, by section 14(1) of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).
Section 190(1)﻿(i): amended, on 28 May 2015, by section 14(2) of the Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters (Social Housing Reform) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 50).
Section 190(3): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Consultation requirements for making regulations

para.text: Before making a recommendation for the making of an Order in Council under section 190, the joint Ministers must be satisfied that the authority has consulted in accordance with subsections (2) and (3).

The authority must do everything reasonably practicable to consult with the persons or organisations that appear to the authority to be representative of the interests of persons likely to be substantially affected by the making of the relevant order.
The process for consultation must, to the extent practicable in the circumstances, include—
giving adequate and appropriate notice of the intention to make the order; and
giving a reasonable opportunity for interested persons to make submissions; and
adequate and appropriate consideration of submissions.
A failure to comply with this section does not affect the validity of any Order in Council made.

history.history-note: Section 191: inserted, on 28 November 2013, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).
Heading: inserted, on 14 April 2014, by section 21 of the Social Housing Reform (Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Amendment) Act 2013 (2013 No 97).

prov.heading: How notice may be given

para.text: Every notice given to any person under this Part may be given by delivering it to that person,—
in the case of a natural person (other than an officer or employee in the service of the Crown in his or her official capacity),—
personally; or
by leaving it at that person's usual or last known place of residence or business or at the address specified by that person in any application or other document received from that person; or
by posting it in a letter addressed to that person at that place of residence or business or at that address; or
by an electronic means of communication to that person that complies with subpart 2 of Part 4 of the Contract and Commercial Law Act 2017:
in the case of any other person, including an officer or employee in the service of the Crown in his or her official capacity,—
where applicable, personally; or
by leaving it at that person's place of business; or
by posting it in a letter addressed to that person at that place of business; or
by an electronic means of communication to that person that complies with subpart 2 of Part 4 of the Contract and Commercial Law Act 2017.

If any such notice is sent to any person by post, then, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, the notice is deemed to have been received by that person on the fourth day after the day on which it is posted, and, in proving the delivery, it is sufficient to prove the letter was properly addressed and posted.
